subject: Mathematics
q: If $\left(2+\frac{x}{3}\right)^{55}$ is expanded in the ascending powers of $x$ and the coefficients of powers of $x$ in two consecutive terms of the expansion are equal, then these terms are:
question: If $\left(2+\frac{x}{3}\right)^{55}$ is expanded in the ascending powers of $x$ and the coefficients of powers of $x$ in two consecutive terms of the expansion are equal, then these terms are:
solution:
Let $r^{\text {th }}$ and $(r+1)^{\text {th }}$ term has equal coefficient
$$
\begin{aligned}
&\left(2+\frac{x}{3}\right)^{55}=2^{55}\left(1+\frac{x}{6}\right)^{55} \\
&r^{\text {th }} \text { term }=2^{5555} C_r\left(\frac{x}{6}\right)^r
\end{aligned}
$$
Coefficient of $x^r$ is $2^{55}{ }^{55} C_r \frac{1}{6^r}$
$$
(r+1)^{\text {th }} \text { term }=2^{5555} C_{r+1}\left(\frac{x}{6}\right)^{r+1}
$$
Coefficient of $x^{r+1}$ is $2^{55}{ }^{55} C_{r+1} \cdot \frac{1}{6^{r+1}}$
Both coefficients are equal
$$
2^{5555} C_r \frac{1}{6^r}=2^{5555} C_{r+1} \frac{1}{6^{r+1}}
$$

$$
\begin{aligned}
&\frac{1}{|r| 55-r}=\frac{1}{\lfloor r+1 \mid 54-r} \cdot \frac{1}{6} \\
&6(r+1)=55-r \\
&6 r+6=55-r \\
&7 r=49 \\
&r=7 \\
&(r+1)=8
\end{aligned}
$$
Coefficient of $7^{\text {th }}$ and $8^{\text {th }}$ terms are equal.

subject: Mathematics
q: Remainder when <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mn>64</mn><msup><mn>32</mn><mn>32</mn></msup></msup></math> is divided by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>9</mn></math> is equal to _____.
question: Remainder when <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mn>64</mn><msup><mn>32</mn><mn>32</mn></msup></msup></math> is divided by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>9</mn></math> is equal to _____.
solution:
Given,

643232=63+132×32×32....32times

⇒643232=63+132λ

Now we know that by binomial theorem,

1+6332λ=C032λ·1+C132λ·1·63+C232λ·1·632+ .......

⇒1+6332λ=1+9α

So, when 643232 is divided by 9 we get 1 as remainder.

subject: Mathematics
q: For natural numbers $m, n$ if $(1-y)^m(1+y)^n=1+a_1 y+a_2 y^2+\ldots$, and $a_1=a_2=10$ then $(\mathrm{m}, \mathrm{n})$ is
question: For natural numbers $m, n$ if $(1-y)^m(1+y)^n=1+a_1 y+a_2 y^2+\ldots$, and $a_1=a_2=10$ then $(\mathrm{m}, \mathrm{n})$ is
solution:
$(1-y)^m(1+y)^n=\left[1-{ }^m C_1 y+{ }^m C_2 y^2-\ldots\right]\left[1+{ }^n C_1 y+{ }^n C_2 y^2+\ldots\right]$
$=1+(n-m)+\left\{\frac{m(m-1)}{2}+\frac{n(n-1)}{2}-m n\right\} y^2+\ldots .$.
$\therefore a_1=n-m=10$ and $a_2=\frac{m^2+n-m-n-2 m n}{2}=10$
So, $n-m=10$ and $(m-n)^2-(m+n)=20 \quad \Rightarrow m+n=80$
$$
\therefore \mathrm{m}=35, \quad \mathrm{n}=45
$$

subject: Mathematics
q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi mathvariant="bold-italic">N</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced close="]" open="["><mi>x</mi></mfenced></math> denote the greatest integer less than or equal to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>.</mo></math> If the sum of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mi>n</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> terms of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mmultiscripts><mi>C</mi><mn>0</mn><none></none><mprescripts></mprescripts><none></none><mi>n</mi></mmultiscripts><mo>,</mo><mn>3</mn><mo>·</mo><mmultiscripts><mi>C</mi><mn>1</mn><none></none><mprescripts></mprescripts><none></none><mi>n</mi></mmultiscripts><mo>,</mo><mn>5</mn><mo>·</mo><mmultiscripts><mi>C</mi><mn>2</mn><none></none><mprescripts></mprescripts><none></none><mi>n</mi></mmultiscripts><mo>,</mo><mn>7</mn><mo>·</mo><mmultiscripts><mi>C</mi><mn>3</mn><none></none><mprescripts></mprescripts><none></none><mi>n</mi></mmultiscripts><mo>,</mo><mo>…</mo></math> is equal to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mn>2</mn><mn>100</mn></msup><mo>·</mo><mn>101</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mfenced close="]" open="["><mfrac><mrow><mi>n</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><mn>2</mn></mfrac></mfenced></math> is equal to
question: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi mathvariant="bold-italic">N</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced close="]" open="["><mi>x</mi></mfenced></math> denote the greatest integer less than or equal to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>.</mo></math> If the sum of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mi>n</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> terms of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mmultiscripts><mi>C</mi><mn>0</mn><none></none><mprescripts></mprescripts><none></none><mi>n</mi></mmultiscripts><mo>,</mo><mn>3</mn><mo>·</mo><mmultiscripts><mi>C</mi><mn>1</mn><none></none><mprescripts></mprescripts><none></none><mi>n</mi></mmultiscripts><mo>,</mo><mn>5</mn><mo>·</mo><mmultiscripts><mi>C</mi><mn>2</mn><none></none><mprescripts></mprescripts><none></none><mi>n</mi></mmultiscripts><mo>,</mo><mn>7</mn><mo>·</mo><mmultiscripts><mi>C</mi><mn>3</mn><none></none><mprescripts></mprescripts><none></none><mi>n</mi></mmultiscripts><mo>,</mo><mo>…</mo></math> is equal to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mn>2</mn><mn>100</mn></msup><mo>·</mo><mn>101</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mfenced close="]" open="["><mfrac><mrow><mi>n</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><mn>2</mn></mfrac></mfenced></math> is equal to
solution:
Let S=C0n+3C1n+5.C2n+…+2n+1·Cnn

Now, Tr=2r+1nCr

Therefore, S=ΣTr

S=Σ2r+1nCr

=Σ2rnCr+ΣnCr

⇒S=2n·2n-1+2n=2nn+1

⇒2nn+1=2100.101

⇒n=100

Now, 2n-12=2992=98.

subject: Mathematics
q: The term independent of <math><mi>x</mi></math> in the expansion of <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>60</mn></mrow></mfrac><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mrow><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>8</mn></mrow></msup></mrow><mrow><mn>81</mn></mrow></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mo>.</mo><msup><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>2</mn><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow><mrow><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></mrow></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mn>6</mn></mrow></msup></math> is equal to
question: The term independent of <math><mi>x</mi></math> in the expansion of <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>60</mn></mrow></mfrac><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mrow><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>8</mn></mrow></msup></mrow><mrow><mn>81</mn></mrow></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mo>.</mo><msup><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>2</mn><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow><mrow><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></mrow></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mn>6</mn></mrow></msup></math> is equal to
solution:
To find the coefficient of term independent of x in given expression, first let's find constant term and coefficient of x-8 in the expansion of  2x2-3x26
Tr+1=  6Cr 2x26-r× -3x2r 
Tr+1=  6Cr 26-r× -3r ×  x12-4r
For constant term:  r=3 , hence constant term =  6C3 23×-33×x0
For coefficient of  x-8 : r=5 , hence coefficient of x-8=  6C5×21-35×x-8
Given expression can be written as
160-x881 6C3×23×-33×x0+…+   6C5×21×-35×x-8+…
Hence, coefficient of term independent of x
=160× 6C3×23-33-181×  6C5×21×-35
= -72+36= -36

subject: Mathematics
q: Let Crn denote the binomial coefficient of xr in the expansion of (1+x)n. If ∑k=01022+3kCkn=α.310+β·210, α, β∈R, then α+β is equal to ___ .
question: Let Crn denote the binomial coefficient of xr in the expansion of (1+x)n. If ∑k=01022+3kCkn=α.310+β·210, α, β∈R, then α+β is equal to ___ .
solution:
Instead of Ckn it must be Ck10 i.e.

∑k=01022+3kCk10=α·310+β·210

LHS=4∑k=010Ck10+3∑k=010k·10k·Ck-19

=4.210+3.10.29

=19.210=α.310+β.210

⇒α=0,β=19⇒α+β=19

subject: Mathematics
q: If the coefficient of the three successive terms in the binomial expansion of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mi>n</mi></msup></math> are in the ratio <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>:</mo><mn>7</mn><mo>:</mo><mn>42</mn></math>, then the first of these terms in the expansion is
question: If the coefficient of the three successive terms in the binomial expansion of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mi>n</mi></msup></math> are in the ratio <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>:</mo><mn>7</mn><mo>:</mo><mn>42</mn></math>, then the first of these terms in the expansion is
solution:
Let coefficient of first term is Crn 

CrnCr+1n=17

⇒ r+1n-r=17

⇒ 7r+7=n-r

n-8r=7       …1 

Also,  Cr+1nCr+2n=742=16

⇒r+2n-r-1=16

⇒6r+12=n-r-1

n-7r=13       …2 

Solving 1 and 2, we get r=6

Hence, 7th term is the answer.

subject: Mathematics
q: Suppose <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>28</mn><mo>-</mo><mi>p</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>p</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>70</mn><mo>-</mo><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>α</mi></math> are the coefficient of four consecutive terms in the expansion of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mi>x</mi><msup><mo>)</mo><mi>n</mi></msup></math>. Then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mi>α</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn><mi>p</mi></math> equals
question: Suppose <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>28</mn><mo>-</mo><mi>p</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>p</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>70</mn><mo>-</mo><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>α</mi></math> are the coefficient of four consecutive terms in the expansion of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mi>x</mi><msup><mo>)</mo><mi>n</mi></msup></math>. Then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mi>α</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn><mi>p</mi></math> equals
solution:
Given,

28-p, p, 70-α, α are the coefficient of four consecutive terms in the expansion of (1+x)n

So, Crn=28-p, Cr+1n=p, Cr+2n=70-α & Cr+3n=α

⇒Crn+Cr+1n=28 & Cr+2n+ Cr+3n=70

⇒Cr+1n+1=28 & Cr+3n+1=70

We know that, C28=28 or C128=28, 

Now on comparing with Cr+1n+1=28 we get, n,r=7,1 or n,r=27,0

Now, checking with Cr+3n+1=70 we get, n,r=7,1

So, p=C27=21 and α=C47=35

 Hence, the value of 2α-3p=70-63=7

q: Let m and n be the coefficients of seventh and thirteenth terms respectively in the expansion of 13x13+12x2318. Then nm13 is:
question: Let m and n be the coefficients of seventh and thirteenth terms respectively in the expansion of 13x13+12x2318. Then nm13 is:
solution:
Given expansion is 13x13+12x2318.

⇒T7=C61813x131212x236

⇒m=C6181312126

⇒T13=C121813x13612x2312

⇒n=C12181361212

⇒mn=C6181312126C12181361212

⇒mn=136126

⇒mn=236

⇒mn13=49

⇒nm13=94

subject: Mathematics
q: The term independent of x in the expansion of x+1x2/3-x1/3+1-x-1x-x1/210  is
question: The term independent of x in the expansion of x+1x2/3-x1/3+1-x-1x-x1/210  is
solution:
We know, x2/3-x1/3+1x1/3+1=x+1

⇒x+1x2/3-x1/3+1=x1/3+1

Also, x-1x-x=x-1x+1xx-1=x+1x=1+x-1/2

Hence, the given binomial becomes x1/3-x-1/210.

General term =10Cr-1rx10-r3x-r2

Hence, for term independent of x.

 We get 10-r3=r2

20-2r=3r

r=4
∴ Term Independent of x=10C4=210

subject: Mathematics
q: If some three consecutive coefficients in the binomial expansion of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mi>n</mi></msup></math> in powers of <math><mi>x</mi></math> are in the ratio <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>:</mo><mn>15</mn><mo>:</mo><mn>70</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then the average of these three coefficients is:
question: If some three consecutive coefficients in the binomial expansion of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mi>n</mi></msup></math> in powers of <math><mi>x</mi></math> are in the ratio <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>:</mo><mn>15</mn><mo>:</mo><mn>70</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then the average of these three coefficients is:
solution:
From the given condition

 nCr:nCr+1:nCr+2=2:15:70

⇒ nCr nCr+1=215 and  nCr+1 nCr+2=1570

⇒ n!n-r!·r!n!n-r-1!·r+1!=215 and  n!n-r-1!·r+1!n!n-r-2!·r+2!=1570

⇒n-r-1!·r+1!n-r!·r!=215 and n-r-2!·r+2!n-r-1!·r+1!=314

⇒n-r-1!·r+1·r!n-r·n-r-1!·r!=215 and n-r-2!·r+2·r+1!n-r-1·n-r-2!·r+1!=314

⇒ r+1n-r=215 and  r+2n-r-1=314

⇒ 17r=2n-15 and 17r=3n-31

⇒3n-31=2n-15, ⇒n=16 and r=1

Hence, average = nCr+nCr+1+nCr+23

= 16C1+16C2+16C33

=232.

subject: Mathematics
q: The number of rational terms in the binomial expansion of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><msup><mn>4</mn><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>4</mn></mfrac></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mn>5</mn><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>6</mn></mfrac></msup></mrow></mfenced><mn>120</mn></msup></math>is_______.
question: The number of rational terms in the binomial expansion of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><msup><mn>4</mn><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>4</mn></mfrac></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mn>5</mn><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>6</mn></mfrac></msup></mrow></mfenced><mn>120</mn></msup></math>is_______.
solution:
We have,

414+516120

Tr+1=Cr120414120-r5r6

⇒Tr+1=Cr1202120-r25r6
 

For rational terms r=6λ, 0≤r≤120, hence

r=0, 6, 12, ..., 120

So, total number of rational terms are 21.

subject: Mathematics
q: If the sum of the coefficients of all the positive powers of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math>, in the binomial expansion of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><msup><mi>x</mi><mi>n</mi></msup><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>5</mn></msup></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mn>7</mn></msup></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>939</mn></math>, then the sum of all the possible integral values of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi></math> is
question: If the sum of the coefficients of all the positive powers of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math>, in the binomial expansion of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><msup><mi>x</mi><mi>n</mi></msup><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>5</mn></msup></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mn>7</mn></msup></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>939</mn></math>, then the sum of all the possible integral values of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi></math> is
solution:
Plotting the table for coefficient of xn+2x57 and there cumulative sum :

Coefficient Commulative sum
x7n→C07 1
x6n-5→2×C17 1+14
x5n-10→22×C27 1+14+84
x4n-15→23×C37 1+14+84+280
x3n-20→24×C47 1+4+84+280+560=939
x2n-25→25×C57  

So, 3n-20≥0 ∩ 2n-25<0 ∩ n∈I

∴  7≤n≤12

Sum =7+8+9+10+11+12=57

subject: Mathematics
q: If the fourth term in the expansion of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>x</mi><mrow><msub><mi>log</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup></mrow></mfenced><mn>7</mn></msup></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4480</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>N</mi></math> is equal to:
question: If the fourth term in the expansion of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>x</mi><mrow><msub><mi>log</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup></mrow></mfenced><mn>7</mn></msup></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4480</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>N</mi></math> is equal to:
solution:
Given x+xlog2x7. 

The general term in the expansion of x+xlog2x7 is Cr7xrxlog2x7-r.

Then, T4=C37x4xlog2x3=4480

⇒7·6·53·2·1x4+log2x=4480

⇒x4+3log2x=128

⇒x4+3log2x=27

Now, from the option only at x=2, LHS=RHS.

subject: Mathematics
q: The sum of the series <math><msup><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>.</mo></mrow><mrow><mn>20</mn></mrow></msup><msub><mrow><mi>C</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>0</mn></mrow></msub><mo>+</mo><msup><mrow><mn>5</mn><mo>.</mo></mrow><mrow><mn>20</mn></mrow></msup><msub><mrow><mi>C</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub><mo>+</mo><msup><mrow><mn>8</mn><mo>.</mo></mrow><mrow><mn>20</mn></mrow></msup><msub><mrow><mi>C</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mo>+</mo><msup><mrow><mn>11</mn><mo>.</mo></mrow><mrow><mn>20</mn></mrow></msup><msub><mrow><mi>C</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msub><mo>+</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>+</mo><msup><mrow><mn>62</mn><mo>.</mo></mrow><mrow><mn>20</mn></mrow></msup><msub><mrow><mi>C</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>20</mn></mrow></msub></math> is equal to
question: The sum of the series <math><msup><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>.</mo></mrow><mrow><mn>20</mn></mrow></msup><msub><mrow><mi>C</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>0</mn></mrow></msub><mo>+</mo><msup><mrow><mn>5</mn><mo>.</mo></mrow><mrow><mn>20</mn></mrow></msup><msub><mrow><mi>C</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub><mo>+</mo><msup><mrow><mn>8</mn><mo>.</mo></mrow><mrow><mn>20</mn></mrow></msup><msub><mrow><mi>C</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mo>+</mo><msup><mrow><mn>11</mn><mo>.</mo></mrow><mrow><mn>20</mn></mrow></msup><msub><mrow><mi>C</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msub><mo>+</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>+</mo><msup><mrow><mn>62</mn><mo>.</mo></mrow><mrow><mn>20</mn></mrow></msup><msub><mrow><mi>C</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>20</mn></mrow></msub></math> is equal to
solution:
Let,

S= 2.20C0+5.20C1+8.20C3+.......+62.20C20              ...i
S=62.20C0+59.20C1+56.20C3+.......+2.20C20       ...ii (Writing in reverse order nCr=nCn-r )
Adding i and ii
2S=64(20C0+20C1+20C2+.....+20C20)
⇒S=32.220=225

q: if the coefficients of three consecutive terms in the expansion of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mi>n</mi></msup></math> are the ratio <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mo>:</mo><mo> </mo><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mo>:</mo><mn>20</mn><mo> </mo></math>then the coefficient of the fourth term is
question: if the coefficients of three consecutive terms in the expansion of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mi>n</mi></msup></math> are the ratio <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mo>:</mo><mo> </mo><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mo>:</mo><mn>20</mn><mo> </mo></math>then the coefficient of the fourth term is
solution:
Given,

Cr-1n:Crn:Cr+1n=1:5:20

Now using the formula CrnCr-1n=n-r+1r we get,

.CrnCr-1n=n-r+1r=51

⇒n-6r+1=0  .........1

And Cr+1nCrn=n-rr+1=205

⇒n-5r-4=0  ..........2

Solving above equations we get,

n=29 & r=5

So, the coefficient of forth term will be C3n=C329=29×28×273×2×1=3654

q: If the coefficient of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>a</mi><mn>7</mn></msup><msup><mi>b</mi><mn>8</mn></msup></math> in the expansion of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi>a</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>b</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>a</mi><mi>b</mi><msup><mo>)</mo><mn>10</mn></msup></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>K</mi><mo>·</mo><msup><mn>2</mn><mn>16</mn></msup><mo>,</mo></math> then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>K</mi></math> is equal to
question: If the coefficient of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>a</mi><mn>7</mn></msup><msup><mi>b</mi><mn>8</mn></msup></math> in the expansion of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi>a</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>b</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>a</mi><mi>b</mi><msup><mo>)</mo><mn>10</mn></msup></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>K</mi><mo>·</mo><msup><mn>2</mn><mn>16</mn></msup><mo>,</mo></math> then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>K</mi></math> is equal to
solution:
Given the expansion  (a+2b+4ab)10

Tr+1 =a10·b101b+2a+410

Tr+1=a10·b10  C10r1+r2 1br12ar2410-r1-r2

Tr+1=a10·b1010!1br12ar2410-r1-r2r1!r2!10-r1-r2!

Putting, r1=2, r2=3

Coefficient of a7b8 is 10!·23·410-2-32!.3!5!

=213·10!2!.3!5!

=216·315

K.216=315..216

Hence, K=315

subject: Mathematics
q: The coefficient of $x^2$ in the expansion of $\left(2x^2 + \dfrac{1}{x}\right)^{10}$, $x \neq 0$, is :
question: The coefficient of $x^2$ in the expansion of $\left(2x^2 + \dfrac{1}{x}\right)^{10}$, $x \neq 0$, is :
solution:
The general term in the expansion of $\left(2x^2 + \dfrac{1}{x}\right)^{10}$ is given by:

$T_{r+1} = ^{10}C_{r} (2x^2)^{10-r} \left(\dfrac{1}{x}\right)^r$

$T_{r+1} = ^{10}C_{r} 2^{10-r} x^{20-2r} x^{-r}$

$T_{r+1} = ^{10}C_{r} 2^{10-r} x^{20-3r}$

For the coefficient of $x^2$, we equate the power of $x$ to $2$:

$20 - 3r = 2$

$3r = 18 \Rightarrow r = 6$

Substituting $r = 6$ in the coefficient part, we get:

Coefficient $= ^{10}C_{6} 2^{10-6}$

$= ^{10}C_{4} 2^4$

$= \dfrac{10 \times 9 \times 8 \times 7}{4 \times 3 \times 2 \times 1} \times 16$

$= 210 \times 16$

$= 3360$

Answer: $3360$

subject: Mathematics
q: Suppose A and B are the coefficients of $30^{\text {th }}$ and $12^{\text {th }}$ terms respectively in the binomial expansion of $(1+x)^{2 \mathrm{n}-1}$. If $2 \mathrm{~A}=5 \mathrm{~B}$, then n is equal to :
question: Suppose A and B are the coefficients of $30^{\text {th }}$ and $12^{\text {th }}$ terms respectively in the binomial expansion of $(1+x)^{2 \mathrm{n}-1}$. If $2 \mathrm{~A}=5 \mathrm{~B}$, then n is equal to :
solution: $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{A}={ }^{2 \mathrm{n}-1} \mathrm{C}_{29} \quad \mathrm{~B}={ }^{2 \mathrm{n}-1} \mathrm{C}_{11} \\ & 2^{2 \mathrm{n}-1} \mathrm{C}_{29}=5{ }^{2 \mathrm{n}-1} \mathrm{C}_{11} \\ & 2 \frac{(2 \mathrm{n}-1)!}{29!(2 \mathrm{n}-30)!}=5 \frac{(2 \mathrm{n}-1)!}{(2 \mathrm{n}-12)!11!} \\ & \frac{1}{29 \ldots 12 \cdot 5}=\frac{1}{(2 \mathrm{n}-12)(2 \mathrm{n}-13) \ldots(2 \mathrm{n}-29) 2} \\ & \frac{1}{30 \cdot 29 \ldots 12}=\frac{1}{(2 \mathrm{n}-12)(2 \mathrm{n}-13) \ldots(2 \mathrm{n}-29) 12} \\ & 2 \mathrm{n}-12=30 \\ & \mathrm{n}=21\end{aligned}$

q: If the coefficients of x7 in ax2+12bx11 and x-7 in ax-13bx211 are equal, then
question: If the coefficients of x7 in ax2+12bx11 and x-7 in ax-13bx211 are equal, then
solution:
The coefficient of x7 in ax2+12bx11 and ax-13bx211 are equal, then

Tr+1=11Crax211-r12bxr

=11Cra11-r12brx22-3r

Now solving 22-3r=7⇒r=5

Coefficient of x-7 in ax-13bx211

Tr+1=11Crax11-r-13bx2r

=11Cra11-r-13brx11-3r

Now solving 11-3r=-7⇒r=6

Now equating coefficient of x7 and x-7 we get,

∴ 11C5a612b5=11C6 a5-13b6

⇒36ab=32

⇒729ab=32

Hence this is the correct option.

question: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>4</mn></mrow></mfenced><mn>10</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><munderover><mo>∑</mo><mrow><mi>r</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></mrow><mn>20</mn></munderover><msub><mi>a</mi><mi>r</mi></msub><msup><mi>x</mi><mi>r</mi></msup><mo>.</mo></math> Then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>7</mn></msub><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>13</mn></msub></mfrac></math> is equal to ______
solution:
General term=10!r1!r2!r3!2x2r13xr24r3

a7=10!.23.3.463!1!6!+10!22.33.452!3!5!+10!.2.35.441!.5!.4!+10!.37.437!3!

a7=2310!.26.3.436!1!3!+10!25.33.425!3!2!+10!.24.35.414!.5!.1!+10!23.37.403!7!

a13=10!.26.3.436!1!3!+10!25.33.425!3!2!+10!.24.35.414!.5!.1!+10!23.37.403!7!

a7a13=2310!.3.2123!1!6!+10!.33.292!.3!.5!+10!.35.261!.5!.4!+10!.37.237!.3!10!.3.2123!.1!.6!+10!.33.292!.3!.5!+10!.35.261!.5!.4!+10!.37.237!.3!=23=8

subject: Mathematics
q: The lowest integer which is greater than <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>100</mn></msup></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>100</mn></msup></msup></math> is
question: The lowest integer which is greater than <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>100</mn></msup></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>100</mn></msup></msup></math> is
solution:
Consider P=1+11010010100,

Let x=10100

⇒P=1+1xx

⇒P=1+x1x+xx-1⌊2·1x2+xx-1x-233·1x3+…

(upto 10100+1 terms)

⇒P=1+1+1⌊2-12x+1⌊3-…+… so on

⇒P=2+(Positive value less than 1⌊2+1⌊3+1⌊4+… )

Also e=1+1⌊1+1⌊2+1⌊3+1⌊4+…

⇒1⌊2+1⌊3+1⌊4+…=e-2

⇒P=2+ (Positive value less than e-2)

⇒P∈2,3

⇒ Least integer value of P is 3.

subject: Mathematics
q: The number of positive integers <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>k</mi></math> such that the constant term in the binomial expansion of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>3</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mn>3</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mi>k</mi></msup></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mn>12</mn></msup><mo>,</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>≠</mo><mn>0</mn></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mn>2</mn><mn>8</mn></msup><mo>·</mo><mi>l</mi></math>, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>l</mi></math> is an odd integer, is ______.
question: The number of positive integers <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>k</mi></math> such that the constant term in the binomial expansion of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>3</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mn>3</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mi>k</mi></msup></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mn>12</mn></msup><mo>,</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>≠</mo><mn>0</mn></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mn>2</mn><mn>8</mn></msup><mo>·</mo><mi>l</mi></math>, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>l</mi></math> is an odd integer, is ______.
solution:
For 2x3+3xk12

Tr+1=Cr122x312-r·3xkr=Cr12·212-r·3r·x36-3r-kr

For the term to be independent of x,

36-3r-kr=0 ⇒r=36k+3 

Here k can be 0,1,3,6,9 for r to be a whole number.

But for Cr12·212-r·3r to be in the form of 28·l

Only k=3, 6 satisfies the above relation.

subject: Mathematics
q:
Let $a=1+\frac{{ }^2 \mathrm{C}_2}{3 !}+\frac{{ }^3 \mathrm{C}_2}{4 !}+\frac{{ }^4 \mathrm{C}_2}{5 !}+\ldots$, $\mathrm{b}=1+\frac{{ }^1 \mathrm{C}_0+{ }^1 \mathrm{C}_1}{1 !}+\frac{{ }^2 \mathrm{C}_0+{ }^2 \mathrm{C}_1+{ }^2 \mathrm{C}_2}{2 !}+\frac{{ }^3 \mathrm{C}_0+{ }^3 \mathrm{C}_1+{ }^3 \mathrm{C}_2+{ }^3 \mathrm{C}_3}{3 !}+\ldots$
Then $\frac{2 b}{a^2}$ is equal to
question:
Let $a=1+\frac{{ }^2 \mathrm{C}_2}{3 !}+\frac{{ }^3 \mathrm{C}_2}{4 !}+\frac{{ }^4 \mathrm{C}_2}{5 !}+\ldots$, $\mathrm{b}=1+\frac{{ }^1 \mathrm{C}_0+{ }^1 \mathrm{C}_1}{1 !}+\frac{{ }^2 \mathrm{C}_0+{ }^2 \mathrm{C}_1+{ }^2 \mathrm{C}_2}{2 !}+\frac{{ }^3 \mathrm{C}_0+{ }^3 \mathrm{C}_1+{ }^3 \mathrm{C}_2+{ }^3 \mathrm{C}_3}{3 !}+\ldots$
Then $\frac{2 b}{a^2}$ is equal to
solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x})=1+\frac{(1+\mathrm{x})}{1 !}+\frac{(1+\mathrm{x})^2}{2 !}+\frac{(1+\mathrm{x})^3}{3 !}+\ldots . . \\
& \frac{\mathrm{e}^{(1+\mathrm{x})}}{1+\mathrm{x}}=\frac{1}{1+\mathrm{x}}+1+\frac{(1+\mathrm{x})}{2 !}+\frac{(1+\mathrm{x})^2}{3 !}+\frac{(1+\mathrm{x})^2}{4 !} \\
& \text { coef } \mathrm{x}^2 \text { in RHS : } 1+\frac{{ }^2 \mathrm{C}_2}{3}+\frac{{ }^3 \mathrm{C}_2}{4}+\ldots=\mathrm{a}
\end{aligned}$
coeff. $x^2$ in L.H.S.
$e\left(1+x+\frac{x^2}{2 !}\right) \ldots\left(1-x+\frac{x^2}{2 !} \ldots \ldots\right)$
is $\mathrm{e}-\mathrm{e}+\frac{\mathrm{e}}{2 !}=\mathrm{a}$
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{b}=1+\frac{2}{1 !}+\frac{2^2}{2 !}+\frac{2^3}{3 !}+\ldots \ldots=\mathrm{e}^2 \\
& \frac{2 \mathrm{~b}}{\mathrm{a}^2}=8
\end{aligned}$

subject: Mathematics
q:
Let $\mathrm{S}_1=\sum_{\mathrm{j}=1}^{10} \mathrm{j}(\mathrm{j}-1)^{10} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{j}}, \mathrm{S}_2=\sum_{\mathrm{j}=1}^{10} \mathrm{j}^{10} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{j}}$ and $\mathrm{S}_3=\sum_{\mathrm{j}=1}^{10} \mathrm{j}^2{ }^{10} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{j}}$.
Statement-1: $\mathrm{S}_3=55 \times 2^9$
Statement-2: $S_1=90 \times 2^8$ and $S_2=10 \times 2^8$.
question:
Let $\mathrm{S}_1=\sum_{\mathrm{j}=1}^{10} \mathrm{j}(\mathrm{j}-1)^{10} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{j}}, \mathrm{S}_2=\sum_{\mathrm{j}=1}^{10} \mathrm{j}^{10} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{j}}$ and $\mathrm{S}_3=\sum_{\mathrm{j}=1}^{10} \mathrm{j}^2{ }^{10} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{j}}$.
Statement-1: $\mathrm{S}_3=55 \times 2^9$
Statement-2: $S_1=90 \times 2^8$ and $S_2=10 \times 2^8$.
options: Statement-1 is true, Statement-2 is true; Statement-2 is not the correct explanation for Statement-1
Statement-1 is true, Statement-2 is false
Statement-1 is false, Statement-2 is true
Statement-1 is true, Statement-2 is true; Statement-2 is the correct explanation for Statement-1

q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mrow><munderover><mo stretchy="true">∑</mo><mrow><mi>i</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><mn>20</mn></munderover><mfenced separators="|"><mfrac><mrow><msup><mi> </mi><mn>20</mn></msup><msub><mi>C</mi><mrow><mi>i</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub></mrow><mrow><msup><mi> </mi><mn>20</mn></msup><msub><mi>C</mi><mi>i</mi></msub><msup><mo>+</mo><mn>20</mn></msup><msub><mi>C</mi><mrow><mi>i</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub></mrow></mfrac></mfenced></mrow><mn>3</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mi>k</mi><mn>21</mn></mfrac><mo>,</mo></math> then <math><mi>k</mi></math> equals
question: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mrow><munderover><mo stretchy="true">∑</mo><mrow><mi>i</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><mn>20</mn></munderover><mfenced separators="|"><mfrac><mrow><msup><mi> </mi><mn>20</mn></msup><msub><mi>C</mi><mrow><mi>i</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub></mrow><mrow><msup><mi> </mi><mn>20</mn></msup><msub><mi>C</mi><mi>i</mi></msub><msup><mo>+</mo><mn>20</mn></msup><msub><mi>C</mi><mrow><mi>i</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub></mrow></mfrac></mfenced></mrow><mn>3</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mi>k</mi><mn>21</mn></mfrac><mo>,</mo></math> then <math><mi>k</mi></math> equals
solution:
Given ∑i=120 20Ci-1 20Ci+ 20Ci-13=k21

We have  20Ci-1 20Ci+ 20Ci-1

Using Cr-1n+Crn=Crn+1, we get

 20Ci-1 20Ci+ 20Ci-1= 20Ci-1 21Ci

Now, using Crn=nr×Cr-1n-1, we get

 20Ci-1 21Ci=20Ci-121i×20Ci-1=i21

Hence, the given sum is 

∑i=120i3213=1213∑i=120i3

Now, using ∑r3r=1n=nn+122, we get

1213∑i=120i3=121320·2122=10021

Using the given sum, we get 10021=k21

⇒k=100.

subject: Mathematics
q: The term independent of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> in the binomial expansion of <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfrac><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>5</mn></mrow></msup></mrow></mfenced><mi> </mi><msup><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>2</mn><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mn>8</mn></mrow></msup></math> is
question: The term independent of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> in the binomial expansion of <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfrac><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>5</mn></mrow></msup></mrow></mfenced><mi> </mi><msup><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>2</mn><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mn>8</mn></mrow></msup></math> is
solution:
The term independent of x will come from

1-1x+3x52x2-1x8

General term of  2x2-1x8 is C 8 r ( − 1 x ) r  2x28-r= C 8 r 2 8−r (−1) r x 16−3r

From expansion

12x2-1x8-1x2x2-1x8+3x52x2-1x8

12x2-1x8→ no constant term

-1x2x2-1x8→ for constant term

 r=5  ⇒ − C 8 3 2 3 (−1) 5 =+448

3x52x2-1x8→ for constant term

r=7⇒ C 38 7 2 1 (−1) 7 =48

Hence, term independent from x=448-48=400

subject: Mathematics
q: If the coefficient of $x^7$ in $\left[a x^2+\left(\frac{1}{b x}\right)\right]^{11}$ equals the coefficient of $x^{-7}$ in $\left[a x^2-\left(\frac{1}{b x}\right)\right]^{11}$, then $a$ and $b$ satisfy the relation
question: If the coefficient of $x^7$ in $\left[a x^2+\left(\frac{1}{b x}\right)\right]^{11}$ equals the coefficient of $x^{-7}$ in $\left[a x^2-\left(\frac{1}{b x}\right)\right]^{11}$, then $a$ and $b$ satisfy the relation
solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
& T_{r+1} \text { in the expansion }\left[a x^2+\frac{1}{b x}\right]^{11}={ }^{11} C_r\left(a x^2\right)^{11-r}\left(\frac{1}{b x}\right)^r \\
& ={ }^{11} C_r(a)^{11-r}(b)^{-r}(x)^{22-2 r-r} \\
& \Rightarrow 22-3 r=7 \Rightarrow r=5 \\
& \therefore \text { coefficient of } x^7={ }^{11} C_5(a)^6(b)^{-5} ....(i)
\end{aligned}
$$
Again $T_{r+1}$ in the expansion $\left[a x-\frac{1}{b x^2}\right]^{11}={ }^{11} C_r(a x)^{11-r}\left(-\frac{1}{b x^2}\right)^r$ $={ }^{11} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}} \mathrm{a}^{11-\mathrm{r}}(-1)^{\mathrm{r}} \times(\mathrm{b})^{-\mathrm{r}}(\mathrm{x})^{-2 \mathrm{r}}(\mathrm{x})^{11-\mathrm{r}}$
Now $11-3 r=-7 \Rightarrow 3 r=18 \Rightarrow r=6$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \therefore \text { coefficient of } x^{-7}={ }^{11} C_6 a^5 \times 1 \times(b)-6 \\
& \Rightarrow{ }^{11} C_5(a)^6(b)^{-5}={ }^{11} C_6 a^5 \times(b)^{-6} \\
& \Rightarrow a b=1
\end{aligned}
$$

subject: Mathematics
q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi></math> is the degree of the polynomial, <math><msup><mrow><mfenced close="]" open="[" separators="|"><mrow><mfrac><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow><mrow><msqrt><mn>5</mn><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></msqrt><mo>-</mo><msqrt><mn>5</mn><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></msqrt></mrow></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mn>8</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><msup><mrow><mfenced close="]" open="[" separators="|"><mrow><mfrac><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow><mrow><msqrt><mn>5</mn><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></msqrt><mo>+</mo><msqrt><mn>5</mn><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></msqrt></mrow></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mn>8</mn></mrow></msup></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>m</mi></math> is the coefficient of <math><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>n</mi></mrow></msup></math> in it, then the ordered pair <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mi>n</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>m</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> is equal to
question: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi></math> is the degree of the polynomial, <math><msup><mrow><mfenced close="]" open="[" separators="|"><mrow><mfrac><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow><mrow><msqrt><mn>5</mn><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></msqrt><mo>-</mo><msqrt><mn>5</mn><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></msqrt></mrow></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mn>8</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><msup><mrow><mfenced close="]" open="[" separators="|"><mrow><mfrac><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow><mrow><msqrt><mn>5</mn><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></msqrt><mo>+</mo><msqrt><mn>5</mn><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></msqrt></mrow></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mn>8</mn></mrow></msup></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>m</mi></math> is the coefficient of <math><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>n</mi></mrow></msup></math> in it, then the ordered pair <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mi>n</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>m</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> is equal to
solution:
25x3+1-5x3-18+ 25x3+1+5x3-18

Rationalizing denominators of both the brackets,

=5x3+1+ 5x3-1  8+ 5x3+1 - 5x3-1  8

=28C0 5x3+18+8C25x3+165x3-12+8C45x3+145x3-14+8C65x3+125x3-16+8C85x3-18

=25x3-14+285x3+135x3-1+705x3+125x3-12+285x3+15x3-13+5x3+14

So, n=12 and 

m=254+140.53+70.54+140.53+54=160000=204
